appliedRows.title: Elon Musk | SpaceX, Tesla, xAI, X, & PayPal | Britannica Money
appliedRows.text: Evidence quote: Elon Musk is a South African-born American entrepreneur who cofounded the electronic-payment firm PayPal and formed SpaceX, maker of launch vehicles and spacecraft. He was also one of the first significant investors in, as well as chief executive officer of, the electric car manufacturer Tesla. In addition he was the owner (and formerly CEO) of the social media company formerly known as Twitter, which he rebranded X. Musk was among the wealthiest people in the world. Even as the wealthiest man in the world, he is still setting new records and raising the bar for what ultra-wealthy looks like. appliedRows.title: SpaceX Tender Offer Pushes Musk’s Net Worth To Record $677 Billion
appliedRows.text: Evidence quote: Elon Musk just took another giant leap toward becoming the world’s first trillionaire. Earlier this month, Musk’s rocket maker SpaceX launched a tender offer valuing the firm at $800 billion... That boosts the fortune of Musk, who owns an estimated 42% of SpaceX. Selection reason: 福布斯权威报道，明确指出马斯克因SpaceX估值飙升而成为首位净资产达6000亿美元的人，直接支持原始声明。 Source preview: This is a BETA experience. opt-out Billionaires Daily Cover Elon Musk Just Became The First Person Ever Worth $600 Billion ByMatt Durot, Forbes Staff. I write about the world’s richest people and their businesses. Updated Dec 15, 2025, 04:24pm EST The world’s richest person just hit another major milestone thanks to the soaring valuation of his rocket maker SpaceX. Elon Musk just took another giant leap toward becoming the world’s first trillionaire. appliedRows.title: Reach 2025: Geoffrey Hinton – CIFAR
appliedRows.text: Evidence quote: When Geoffrey Hinton first joined CIFAR in 1987, artificial neural networks – computer systems modeled on the human brain and nervous system – were met with doubt that they would ever work. But Hinton saw a different future. Selection reason: CIFAR官方出版物，详细介绍了Hinton与CIFAR的长期关系及其贡献，直接支持人物与机构关联。 Source preview: CIFAR header logo menu_mobile_logo_alt Follow Us Reach 2025: Geoffrey Hinton By Liz Do The long game of AI, and a Nobel Prize to show for it Headshot of Hinton When Geoffrey Hinton first joined CIFAR in 1987, artificial neural networks – computer systems modeled on the human brain and nervous system – were met with doubt that they would ever work. But Hinton saw a different future. “My main message is, if you want to do important basic research, you should look for something where you think people are doing it wrong.” He says. “You shouldn’t give up on your belief until you understand why you are wrong.” CIFAR saw a different future as well. With the longstanding belief that advancing funda...
